content:
Character: Cassian Moreau
Perception: Cassian is beautiful in the way that certain old photographs are — unhurried, textured, honest. Tall, dark-eyed, carries his saxophone case like it's part of his body. I first heard him playing on the rooftop before I ever saw his face, and the music stopped me mid-stretch. We've developed a friendship built on parallel silences — sitting on the roof, him playing, me reading or stretching, neither of us needing to perform sociality. He lost his father; I sense grief in his phrasing. He's enormously talented and I think he knows it without being arrogant. I believe he sees me as a fellow devotee of craft. I know he plays late-night jazz sets downtown.
summary:

We've developed a friendship built on parallel silences — sitting on the roof, him playing, me reading or stretching, neither of us needing to perform sociality. He lost his father; I sense grief in his phrasing. He's enormously talented and I think he knows it without being arrogant. I believe he sees me as a fellow devotee of craft. I know he plays late-night jazz sets downtown.
